attacking_style: Extreme long-range breakaway specialist, capable of sustaining efforts over 200km+ from very early in the stage. Excels at holding slim margins (20 seconds) against organized chase efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with significant autonomy in breakaways, relying on personal endurance rather than team support once in the move. Team allows early freedom for stage hunting opportunities.
terrain_preference: Ultra-long stages where attrition and sustained tempo favor diesel engines over explosive climbers. Can survive on final climbs when GC teams fracture the chase group.
vulnerability: Vulnerable to well-organized team chases (Mitchelton-Scott reduced 15-minute advantage significantly). Relies on chaos in peloton rather than raw climbing power on decisive ascents.
key_patterns: Commits to breakaways from extreme distance, riding 200km+ off the front in Grand Tours.
Survives on narrow margins when caught late, holding off chasers by seconds rather than minutes.
Benefits from GC team attacks fragmenting pursuit, converting chaos into survival opportunities.
form_trajectory: positive: Demonstrated exceptional endurance and tactical awareness to convert 200km+ breakaway into stage victory at 2019 Giro
